tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset VERSION_CONTROL
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX=.bak "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if ! grep -qx 'old' "$tmpdir/dst.bak"; then
  echo "SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX backup not created with old contents" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX sets the backup suffix when --backup makes a simple backup
tests.expected: The existing destination is backed up using the suffix from SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset VERSION_CONTROL
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX=.env "$UTIL" --backup -S .cmd "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if ! grep -qx 'old' "$tmpdir/dst.cmd"; then
  echo "-S did not override SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: -S/--suffix overrides the usual backup suffix, including SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
tests.expected: The backup uses the suffix supplied by -S, not the environment suffix.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
VERSION_CONTROL=none "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if [[ -e "$tmpdir/dst~" || -e "$tmpdir/dst.~1~" ]]; then
  echo "VERSION_CONTROL=none created a backup" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=none: documented to never make backups even if --backup is given
tests.expected: No simple or numbered backup file is created.

printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
VERSION_CONTROL=off "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if [[ -e "$tmpdir/dst~" || -e "$tmpdir/dst.~1~" ]]; then
  echo "VERSION_CONTROL=off created a backup" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=off alias for none: documented to never make backups even if --backup is given
tests.expected: No simple or numbered backup file is created.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
VERSION_CONTROL=numbered "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if ! grep -qx 'old' "$tmpdir/dst.~1~"; then
  echo "VERSION_CONTROL=numbered did not create numbered backup" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.expected: The old destination is saved as the first numbered backup.

VERSION_CONTROL=t "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
VERSION_CONTROL=existing "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if ! grep -qx 'old' "$tmpdir/dst~"; then
  echo "VERSION_CONTROL=existing without numbered backups did not make simple backup" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=existing: simple backup when no numbered backups exist
tests.expected: With no numbered backup already present, the old destination is saved as a simple backup.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'prior\n' > "$tmpdir/dst.~1~"
VERSION_CONTROL=existing "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if ! grep -qx 'old' "$tmpdir/dst.~2~"; then
  echo "VERSION_CONTROL=existing with numbered backup present did not make next numbered backup" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=existing: numbered backup if numbered backups exist
tests.expected: With an existing numbered backup, the old destination is saved as the next numbered backup.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'prior\n' > "$tmpdir/dst.~1~"
VERSION_CONTROL=nil "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if ! grep -qx 'old' "$tmpdir/dst.~2~"; then
  echo "VERSION_CONTROL=nil did not behave like existing with numbered backups present" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=nil alias for existing: numbered backup if numbered backups exist
tests.expected: With an existing numbered backup, the old destination is saved as the next numbered backup.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'prior\n' > "$tmpdir/dst.~1~"
VERSION_CONTROL=simple "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if ! grep -qx 'old' "$tmpdir/dst~"; then
  echo "VERSION_CONTROL=simple did not make a simple backup" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=simple always makes simple backups, even when numbered backups exist
tests.expected: The old destination is saved as a simple backup.

tests.body:

set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
printf 'prior\n' > "$tmpdir/dst.~1~"
VERSION_CONTROL=never "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if ! grep -qx 'old' "$tmpdir/dst~"; then
  echo "VERSION_CONTROL=never did not behave like simple" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: VERSION_CONTROL=never alias for simple: always make simple backups
tests.expected: The old destination is saved as a simple backup.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
VERSION_CONTROL=numbered "$UTIL" --backup=simple "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if ! grep -qx 'old' "$tmpdir/dst~"; then
  echo "--backup=simple did not override VERSION_CONTROL=numbered" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: Version control method may be selected via --backup option or VERSION_CONTROL; explicit --backup=CONTROL selects simple over env numbered
tests.expected: The explicit --backup=simple control creates a simple backup despite VERSION_CONTROL=numbered.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
VERSION_CONTROL=numbered "$UTIL" "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst"
if [[ -e "$tmpdir/dst~" || -e "$tmpdir/dst.~1~" ]]; then
  echo "VERSION_CONTROL alone caused a backup without --backup" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --backup is the option that makes backups; VERSION_CONTROL only selects the method
tests.expected: No backup file is created when --backup is absent, even with VERSION_CONTROL set.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
VERSION_CONTROL=bogus "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "invalid VERSION_CONTROL value succeeded" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: VERSION_CONTROL is documented to accept only the listed control values
tests.expected: An unlisted VERSION_CONTROL value is rejected with nonzero exit status.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
unset VERSION_CONTROL
unset SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" --backup=bogus "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "invalid --backup control value succeeded" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --backup[=CONTROL] is documented with a finite set of CONTROL values
tests.expected: An unlisted --backup control value is rejected with nonzero exit status.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" --update=none-fail "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "--update=none-fail skipped existing destination without failure" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update=none-fail: skipped existing destination files are diagnosed and induce a failure
tests.expected: Moving onto an existing destination with --update=none-fail exits nonzero.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dst"
set +e
"$UTIL" --update=bogus "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dst" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e
if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "invalid --update argument succeeded" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.expected: An unlisted --update argument is rejected with nonzero exit status.
